title: sabulina tenuifolia
intro: Sabulina tenuifolia, also known as the desert lavender, is a flowering plant species native to the southwestern United States and northern Mexico. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antimicrobial properties. The health benefits of Sabulina tenuifolia include its potential to soothe skin irritations, reduce fever, and ease respiratory issues, such as coughs and colds. Traditional herbal preparations of Sabulina tenuifolia include teas, infusions, and salves, which can be made by infusing the flowers in hot water or mixing the essential oils with a carrier oil.
